Tests object addition with NaN in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that tests the behavior of adding a JavaScript `Object` instance to `NaN` (Not-a-Number). It creates a new object `obj` and uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of `obj + NaN` and `NaN + obj` both evaluate to `NaN`. The script demonstrates the use of the `isNaN` function to check if the result of these operations is `NaN`, ensuring that the addition of an object and `NaN` does not produce a valid number. This code provides narrow functionality focused on validating specific arithmetic behavior in JavaScript.
